JUST my experience, I know there are many who LOVE it.

reviews.title: DOES NOT STAY ON IN HOT & HUMID WEATHER!
reviews.text:
I was thrilled to receive this product, and yes, I got a legitimate one, not a knock off. And I was ecstatic at how easy it was to apply and how well it concealed my dark under eye circles and other blemishes. Well my happiness was short lived. I had just finished my makeup and was about to head out for the evening when I looked back in the mirror, and to my horror (and soul-crushing disappointment,) saw that it was, for lack of a better word, dissolving in the areas I’d applied it—especially under my eyes. There were bald patches and a ton of seperation.
I had applied a matifying primer before using the concealer, and I applied a good setting powder on top. I had to remove my full face of makeup and start fresh with my old concealer. I was quite pissed, to be perfectly honest! I live in the south where it’s hot and humid. If you’re thinking of buying this product for the first time (and live in a humid area,) you may want to wait until fall or winter. I gave this product a generous 3 stars because it did apply and conceal fabulously, before it all went horribly wrong. And the only reason I’m not returning it for a full refund is that I want to try it again once the weather cools down. I sincerely hope it doesn’t fail me a second time. And on a side note, I would’ve been positively mortified if it had broken down once I was out in public, with no way to fix my face. It was that bad.

I'm 51. Haven't seen my natural hair color - not more than 2.5" during pandemic, anyway - since I was 19. My natural color is such an extreme ash, it's literally not even a color it's just ash. If 'light charcoal BLONDE' was a color, that'd be mine. And weirdly enough, adding any bleach to my unique natural color would always bring out the red that apparently hides deeeeeeeeeep within (from my daddy's sisters, both redheads). I've used all the toners, all the hair colors, embraced the reds, desperately tried to remove them with every product sold in salons, salon suppliers, Sally's, etc. had every shade of blonde from platinum to dark ultra ash, several shades of brown, you name it. My hair has definitely been good to me, never leaving me no matter how much I abused it 😅 But I am at that stage in my life where I just want to embrace natural color before it turns silver and white. My current color was the dark blonde, very close to my natural color except no amount of product was taking the red out and restoring the world's ashiest ash.

Note: I was nervous and some parts of my hair didn't get left on as long as others. BUT I am so so so so so so happy!! I believe this is as close as it will get while I leave it to grow out now. I did add 1:1 water to the developer, to lessen any bleaching. Again, I was attempting a tint/tone and not a color.

Scent is typical of any other box color, still floral just not as yuck and heavy as others.

reviews.text:
After seeing this brow pencil in the Better Homes and Gardens Magazine I decided to get one and try it out. I was interested in a new pencil since the one I've been using for years finally was running out.

This eyebrow pencil is easy-to-use and is the perfect size for defining eyebrows. You can pencil in missing hairs in places where your eyebrows have become a bit sparse. Then the miniature bottle brush (that is what it looks like) evens out the color and shapes the eyebrows perfectly. I am impressed! The brush almost seems intelligent in how it shapes the hair of your eyebrows into place.

Get the shade that looks closest to your hair color and it should be perfect. I got the medium brown and it matches my hair color perfectly. There is also no need to sharpen the pencil which is a bonus.

I feel like the way my eyebrows looked after I used this gave me a boost of confidence. You really will feel like a model who has a professional makeup artist! It makes my eyes look more open and pretty. If you are looking to enhance and frame your eyes, you can't go wrong with this. I'm glad I took the chance and it was easy to find here at amazon where I now buy most of my makeup.

~The Rebecca Review

reviews.title: Good starter brow pencil
reviews.text: This is a great brow pencil for fair-skinned and light-haired people.  The color it adds to the brows is subtle.  It's enough to give definition, but not enough to make it look like you've added color to your brows.  I'm blonde and my brows are so light that they tend to disappear into my face.  This pencil definitely helps correct that.  Application is easy- the pencil part twists up to reveal the color.  As the instructions state, you brush it on in short strokes, like you were drawing brows.  Then, use the spoolie on the other end to blend.  I found the blending to be good and the results looked quite natural.  Even if you don't know what you're doing and don't have a set technique, the end result looks decent.  The only complaint I have is that the color gets used up fairly quickly, so if you do your brows every day, you'll have to replace frequently.

reviews.title: quick and easy, fine results
reviews.text: There are more high tech ways to do it, with kits that have fine tipped pens with multiple strokes to look like little hairs, or combos of pencil and powder, etc. This is just a low-key quick fix that stays put. It’s like eyeliner that’s a little more solid and much more fine-tipped, so you can scratch on little lines toward the inside of your hairs. Is this good for just drawing on brows onto a shaved surface? Nope. But if you are just shaping existing brows and staying in the lines, this adds definition and strength without coming off in rain or with sweat. If you are just looking to fill in a bit or make them look bolder, this works!

reviews.title: As a woman, I love the price
reviews.text:
I love this. This is my second time buying some and it's cheaper here for some reason. I will probably do a sub & save. I had been using a women's brand of root coverup but... the price went up this does the same thing for cheaper.

PROS: As I said, I have used this brand before & IMO, it lasts as long (or longer) than my ladies' brand.
The tube looks small (about the size of a travel toothpaste) & I was worried that I would only get one or 2 uses but I think I can get at least 4 uses (maybe more) because it's just for touchups. The first tube I used, I was able to get three uses and my hair was a lot longer then. I really like some things about the "brush" on this one better because it's easier for targeting only the grays than with the long & really soft brushes I've used before.

By the way, the "brush" is not soft but is HARD plastic. you don't "paint" this dye on like with some products but literally pull the brush through your hair. I have afro-natural hair so I had to be gentle but I got the hang of it quickly.

There is no mixing. The dye is ready to use as soon as you attach the brush. It's easy to use just a little cream at a time and then gently dispense a little more as you need it so you aren't wasting the product.

The color came out great and covered all the grays. The dark brown is nice and rich and matches my almost-black hair perfectly.

CONS: The gloves are those awful plastic-y kinds & are not easy to clean for re-use. I throw them out after 1 use & go back to my own latex gloves.

Cleaning the brush afterward is a bit tricky because the brush is hard, not soft. Also, the bristles are a bit too thick & would be perfect if they were just a little thinner - like on a comb. I had to run really hot water for a bit to get all the residue out. It would be nice if there was a cap to just cover the brush in between uses. As it is, I had to remove and clean the brush & then get the cap back on until the next time. Since there is nowhere but the original box to store the brush, I just keep it in a small baggie until I use it again.

Luckily I only need to do the front and sides of my hair. Doing the very top and back would be tricky without help. That's the same with any root touch up but I would have to be really careful because of the hard brush.

Overall, I really like this and it's probably going to be my go-to dye when I don't want to bother with henna. This is like the whole razor blades dilemma: they do the same thing but pink ones cost more! LOL

reviews.title: Very strong product- learning curve to be aware of especially if the forms lift before curing.
reviews.text:
I am a former nail tech and worked primarily with building gels before. I had some experience with acrylic but preferred gel because of the lack of smell. This is not as easy for beginners i feel because you need to understand how to work with the products- especially if problems arise and none of the videos I watched showed the difficulties! I even searched for it specifically...After filling the form and applying it to the prepped nail, etc- the form wouldn’t stay down on 3-4 of my nails on each hand. It would start to lift up...creating bubbles, hollow spots etc, so I had to hold it down which was awkward and cumbersome. Once I knew I had to do that- things went pretty well.
The product itself is strong and with a well prepped nail bed- it stays a long time. I wish the tubs were bigger and that they came with nail form stickers too. Oh and you’re going to need an efile for this. I recommend their 30,000 rpm drill and then get the carbide drill bits for it. The ones that come with it aren’t strong enough.

reviews.title: Just ok all considering
reviews.text:
I got the color red. First of the smell of this is not bad. Unlike some hair coloring or hair tinting products that can smell pretty bad due to the chemicals this is actually not bad. They added some nice scents in it to cover up the chemicals they used for the color.

When you use it I decided to massage it into my wet hair, lather it up well and let it sit for like 3 to 5 minutes, mostly because I currently don't have my hair freshly colored and they say this will work best if you have your hair color and to keep the color more lively. That is why I decided to let it sit for a little instead of apply, massage in, lather and rinse which are the instructions. Btw, writing them a little bigger would have been nice since apparently my eye sight is going just a tad and reading this in the shower without glasses is not optimal. Anyways, the result was actually not bad. There was a hint of red in my hair and my hair did not have a chemical smell, but I did use a leave in conditioner afterwards so that could be the reason for that. It was nowhere near coloring your hair but I think if you keep using it you could intensify this, and I will try that. BUT I think it would be easier to just color it with temporary color instead and it would also be cheaper in my eyes instead of using this $35 shampoo! (most hair dye is like $5-$8 and even if you get semi permanent hair dye it will last at least 4 weeks!)

Lastly, when using it it was as bad almost as using real hair dye. My hands were deep red and it took quite a bit of rinsing and washing to get all this color of my hands. and of course I found out after I got out of the shower that my hairline had some colored shampoo that made a mess so that took a bit now to clean up since I let it sit for a few minutes. So make sure that you clean your hairline with water after you have the shampoo in there or you will have discolored skin. With that being said this can and will also stain your tiles, so make sure to rinse them right away.

So considering everything, it is not the most user friendly since most don't have a mirror in their bathroom to check they are not making a mess of themselves, does not provide a massive color boost which could be more easily achieved with just coloring instead when it starts to fade, which for most semi permanent dyes I found happens around 4 weeks, and considering the price of this at right now $35, I just give this a m3 stars. For that price I would expect more from this product.

reviews.title: Fashion color veteran
reviews.text:
I wear fashion colors all the time but I get tired of them fading in-between visits to my professional colorist. This time my hair is a deep red with light tips that we did in this color. Because it fades, I ordered this to keep up on it until I get to see her again. I have used brands like Overtone and Keracolor Clenditioner but you sort of end up needing to go with brands that are in your color at the time.

Keep in mind that if you have dark hair and you want these to show up, you'll have to get your hair bleached first. That's just how it works with these colors. Also, you have to wash your hair in cold water and avoid heat as much as possible. When I wear fashion colors, I only wash my hair once a week in cold water to help maintain them. I also try to only apply heat once a week when I curl it for the week. I just try to style it based on how my curls hold throughout the rest of the week.

I really like this brand. I apply it on dry hair, wrap it up with seran wrap and foil before wearing it for as long as I want and rinsing in cold water. Make sure you wear gloves and avoid the skin around your face, especially with dark colors.

The scent is wonderful. The color went on easily, and looked fabulous. It did NOT adhere well to my roots. It was grown out about an inch and a half. It adhered well to the lengths that had been lightened a little before.

I used Neon Peach for most of my head, and purple for my bangs that sweep back over my head. It looks amazing.

reviews.title: So pretty
reviews.text:
I have dark (but lightened to an 7/8) wavy, thick, shoulder-length hair. I chose Chocolate Cherry to try. This dye is easy to use, it's texture is comparable to Arctic Fox or Manic Panic. It applied evenly with gloved hands. After sitting for 2 hours it had hardened up but washed out with no issues in the shower. The dye did stain my hands and nails just from washing it out ungloved, this faded after a couple days. The color is vibrant, a little brighter than I was expecting, but I have gotten SO many compliments. Indoors my hair looks dark reddish purple, outside it is a BRIGHT purplish red. I did have a tiny bit of pastel pink left in my hair, so I attribute some of the brightness to that.
I have already washed and heat styled my hair twice since dying it and the color is unchanged. My hair did feel a bit dry after washing out the dye, I used an argan-infused conditioner and some oils afterward and it made a huge difference. Also, I only used half of the bottle, which is awesome!

I uploaded photos in different lights, the photo where I'm in the hoodie is from the day I dyed it, others are from today.
I will try to update how long/how many washes until the color has noticeably changed.

title: Just For Men Control GX Grey Reducing Beard Wash Shampoo, Gradually Colors Mustache and Beard, Leaves Facial Hair Softer and Fuller, 4 Fl Oz - Pack of 1
description: Control GX is the first and only shampoo smart enough to gradually reduce greys by slowly bringing them back to their natural shade while it cleanses and conditions your hair. You get an extremely natural look - just by shampooing as you normally do. Just use it instead of your usual shampoo until you like what you see. Then use it in rotation with your usual shampoo to maintain the look you like. There's never been an easier way to control your grey. Get the look you want: Use the control GX as your daily shampoo, and after the first few washes, you'll start to see a subtle reduction in the amount of gray. Continue to use it until you reach your desired level of grey reduction (generally within 2 to 4 weeks). You can even control how gradually you get to your desired result by using it daily or every other day at the start. Keep it that way: Once you've reached the level of grey reduction you like, you can reduce the frequency of use, alternating with another shampoo. In general guys use it as their daily shampoo at the start, and when they are in "maintenance mode" they use it 3 to 4 times a week. CGX Innovative beard color shampoo gradually reduces grey with every wash. Gently and thoroughly cleans and revitalizes, increasing volume for a thicker, fuller look. Reduces grey beard hairs gradually, for subtle, natural-looking results, unlike other dyes. No ammonia or peroxide. Triple patented formula, keeps beard hair looking stronger and healthier. This product works best for all shades of hair. Makes reducing gray easy: All you have to do is wash your beard Also available in shampoo, shampoo for light shades, and anti-dandruff shampoo Just use it instead of your beard wash until you like what you see. Then alternate with your regular beard wash to maintain the look you like.
